task_completion.description: Checks if the bay_area_water_stations CSV was loaded into the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel in the GIS software (e.g., QGIS) shows 'bay_area_water_stations' as an active layer.

task_completion.description: Checks if the water station points are displayed on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map canvas displays point features (exactly 8 points) representing the water monitoring stations.

task_completion.description: Checks if the Voronoi/Thiessen polygon tool was accessed and configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A processing dialog box for 'Voronoi Polygons' or 'Thiessen Polygons' is open, with the water stations point layer selected as the input.

task_completion.description: Checks if the Voronoi polygons were successfully created and rendered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map canvas shows a network of adjoining convex polygons (Voronoi cells) that partition the space around the points, and a new polygon layer is visible in the Layers panel.

task_completion.description: Checks if the original station attributes were preserved in the new polygon layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute table or feature identification panel for the newly generated Voronoi polygon layer is open, displaying columns such as 'station_name', 'ph', and 'dissolved_oxygen_mg_l'.

task_completion.description: Checks if the polygon layer was exported to the correct GeoJSON file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog shows the format set to GeoJSON and the destination path as '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/voronoi_zones.geojson', OR a terminal/file manager shows this specific file was created in the correct directory.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used appropriate GIS tools rather than faking the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIS software (e.g., QGIS) menus, processing toolboxes, or a legitimate spatial Python script, not just a text editor creating a mock file.

integrity.description: Verifies the generated polygons are mathematically genuine Voronoi cells.
integrity.visual_evidence: The polygons form a continuous tessellation where each cell contains exactly one point, rather than arbitrary hand-drawn shapes, circles, or grids.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't just download or copy-paste a pre-made GeoJSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual history shows the progression from importing points to generating polygons to exporting, without the sudden appearance of the final file from a web browser or clipboard paste.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input CSV is located at '/home/ga/GIS_Data/bay_area_water_stations.csv'. Expected output is a GeoJSON file at '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/voronoi_zones.geojson'. The output must contain exactly 8 features with 'Polygon' geometry. The final exported polygons must preserve the attributes from the input CSV, specifically including 'station_name', 'ph', and 'dissolved_oxygen_mg_l'.
